content: The cognitive self-reflection loop at the core of the standalone Reflexion autonomous agent framework can borrow the multi-perspective inconsistency sampling approach from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to fix a critical flaw: the tendency of single-perspective self-reflection to get stuck in repeated, uncorrected error cycles. Just as multi-perspective reflection on inconsistent solution attempts helps LLMs correct flawed reasoning, inconsistent self-reflection perspectives drawn from the agent's dynamic memory can help it avoid overfitting to its own earlier incorrect outputs and escape local optima in hard search or reasoning tasks.
